docs.title: Computer Science
docs.text:
• Data Structures
  • Algorithms
  • Misc.

docs.title: Bits
docs.text: Size Description Bit 0 or 1 Byte 8 bits Megabyte(MB) 1 million or 220 bytes. Gigabyte(GB) 1 billion or 230 bytes.

docs.title: Java Memory Usage
docs.text:
Size Description int 4 bytes double 8 bytes Object reference 8 bytes Array 24 bytes + memory for each array entry Object 16 bytes + memory for each instance variable + 8 if inner class (pointer)

If array entry or instance variable is a reference, add memory (recursively) for referenced object.

docs.title: Recursion
docs.text:
• Base Case: A conditional statement as the first statement that has a return.
  • Recursive calls must address sub-problems. Meaning they must be smaller problems that converge to the base case.
  • Recursive calls should not overlap.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
• Binary Search
  • Tree traversal

docs.title: Queue
docs.text:
Process by least recently added.

FIFO: First In First Out

docs.title: Operations
docs.text:
• Queue (Insert)
  • Enqueue (Remove)
  • Iterate
  • isEmpty

docs.title: API
docs.text:
classDiagram
class Queue~Item~{
    +enqueue(Item item): void
    +dequeue(): Item
    +isEmpty(): boolean
    +size(): int
}

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
Process by most recently added.

LIFO: Last In First Out

Can be represented in various ways (Array/Linked List), with Linked List being the most common.

docs.title: API
docs.text:
classDiagram
class Stack~Item~{
    +push(Item item): void
    +pop(): Item
    +isEmpty(): boolean
    +size(): int
}

docs.title: Node
docs.text:
Data structure used to create a linked list.

classDiagram
class Node{
    +Item item
    +Node next
}

docs.title: Linked List
docs.text: A recursive data structure that is either empty (null) or a "node" containing a value and a reference to a linked list.

docs.title: Building A Linked List
Operations
docs.text:
Using the 3-node example above

Insert At The BeginningRemove From The BeginningInsert At The EndTraverse A Linked List 
    Node oldFirst = first; 
    first = new Node(); 
    first.item = "not";
    first.next = oldFirst; 

    first = first.next;

    Node oldLast = last;
    last = new Node();
    last.item = "not";
    oldLast.next = last;

    // Please note this only works if the Linked List extends the Iterable interface.
    for (Node x = first; x != null; x = x.next) {
       //process 
    }

docs.title: Array
docs.text:
A collection of elements stored at a contiguous memory location.

  • Fixed size

docs.title: Operations
docs.text:
Create 

    int[] arr = new int[5];


    int[] arr = {1,2,3,4,5};

    int[] arr = new int[1];

Assign 

    arr[0] = 99;

Access 

    arr[0];

Modify Element 

    arr[0] = 2;

Multi-Dimensional Array 

    int[][] arr = new int[5][5];
